quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 14 Quiz 1
quiz.subtitle: 14.1: Matrix Operations: Addition, Subtraction, Scalar Multiplication
quiz.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If matrix $A$ is $3 \times 2$ and matrix $B$ is $2 \times 3$, what is the result of $A + B$?
questions.explanation: Since a $3 \times 2$ matrix and a $2 \times 3$ matrix do not have the same dimensions, they cannot be added.

questions.question: Given $A = \begin{bmatrix} 4 & -2 & 7 \\ 1 & 0 & -5 \end{bmatrix}$, what is the value of $a_{23}$?
questions.explanation: $a_{ij}$ is the element at row $i$, column $j$. $a_{23}$ = row 2, column 3 = $-5$.

questions.question: Perform scalar multiplication: $3 \times \begin{bmatrix} 2 & -1 \\ 0 & 4 \end{bmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: Multiply every element by the scalar 3.

questions.question: If $2A = \begin{bmatrix} 10 & -4 \\ 6 & 0 \end{bmatrix}$, what is matrix $A$?
questions.explanation: Divide every element in the matrix by 2.

questions.question: Calculate $A + B$ where $A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 2 \end{bmatrix}$ and $B = \begin{bmatrix} -1 \\ 5 \end{bmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: $1 + (-1) = 0$ and $2 + 5 = 7$. The result is a $2 \times 1$ column matrix.

scoreMessages.90: Perfect! You have a solid understanding of basic matrix arithmetic — addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, identifying elements, solving matrix equations, and combining operations like $2A + B$.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Key reminders: (1) Matrices must have the same dimensions for addition/subtraction, (2) $a_{ij}$ means row $i$, column $j$, (3) Scalar multiplication multiplies every element, (4) Matrix equations are solved by equating corresponding elements.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Careful sign handling when subtracting, (2) Performing scalar multiplication before addition/subtraction, (3) Checking dimensions before attempting operations, (4) Reading matrix notation correctly — rows first, then columns.
scoreMessages.0: Review matrix basics: (1) A matrix is defined by rows × columns, (2) Addition/subtraction: only possible with same dimensions — add element-wise, (3) Scalar multiplication: multiply every element by the scalar, (4) Matrix equality: corresponding elements must be equal.
